description: PFC 5.0 command documentation extracted from pfchelp.chm

commands.name: attribute
commands.short_description: Force applied to balls

commands.name: create
commands.short_description: Ball ID. If not specified, then the next available ID is used. An error is raised if a ball with the specified ID already exists in the system

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete balls. If no range is specified, then all balls in the model are deleted.

commands.name: distribute
commands.short_description: Specify the radius range to be used during generation

commands.name: export
commands.short_description: Resolution of the facet edges

commands.name: extra
commands.short_description: Set ball extra variables.

commands.name: fix
commands.short_description: The angular velocity is fixed

commands.name: free
commands.short_description: The angular velocity is free

commands.name: generate
commands.short_description: Ball will fully fall within this box, such that they will not overlap the box faces or edges. By default, the box is the model domain

commands.name: group
commands.short_description: Specify ball group names.

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Magnitude of the force resulting from contacts

commands.name: initialize
commands.short_description: Modify ball attributes.

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List ball attributes

commands.name: list ball
commands.short_description: List ball information.

commands.name: property
commands.short_description: Assign ball surface properties.

commands.name: result
commands.short_description: Activate the creation of ball result states in memory or as files

commands.name: tolerance
commands.short_description: Assign the constant contact-detection tolerance. This is assigned on a per-ball basis

notes: ball attribute = ball state; ball property = contact surface properties (transferred to contacts)

commands.name: activeside
commands.short_description: Set both sides as active

commands.name: addfacet
commands.short_description: ID of the facet that is being added

commands.name: attribute
commands.short_description: Set the center of rotation for walls in the range. This is the position about which rotations occur when an angular velocity is supplied to a wall. The center of rotation translates with the wall if a translational velocity is supplied. The default center of rotation is the origin

commands.name: convey
commands.short_description: The point about which the calculation of the tangential velocity at the facet centroid is calculated

commands.name: create
commands.short_description: Wall ID. If not specified, then the next available ID is used

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete facets. Walls without facets are deleted

commands.name: export
commands.short_description: Exports walls. If no range is specified, then all walls in the model are exported. At the moment, it is only possible to export walls to geometry sets.

commands.name: extra
commands.short_description: Set wall or facet extra variables.

commands.name: generate
commands.short_description: Wall ID. If not specified, then the next available ID is used

commands.name: group
commands.short_description: Specify wall or facet group names.

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Magnitude of the force resulting from contacts

commands.name: import
commands.short_description: Name of the file from which to import

commands.name: initialize
commands.short_description: Modify wall attributes.

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List wall attributes

commands.name: property
commands.short_description: Assign facet surface properties.

commands.name: resolution
commands.short_description: The contact resolution procedure for contacts with facets is at its full state. This means that in addition to the associative checks for contact activity, the sliver penetration conditions are checked, and the cutoff angle becomes active for contact-state information propagation for convex and concave facet intersections. Proximity contacts may result

commands.name: result
commands.short_description: Activate the creation of wall result states in memory or as files

commands.name: rotate
commands.short_description: Axis of rotation

commands.name: servo
commands.short_description: Set the servo-control active status

commands.name: tolerance
commands.short_description: Assign the constant contact-detection tolerance. This is assigned on a per-facet basis

notes: Wall facets default active=0 in PFC 5.0; use 'wall activeside' to enable

commands.name: activate
commands.short_description: Change the contact activate flag.

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete contacts in specified range.

commands.name: extra
commands.short_description: Set contact extra variables.

commands.name: group
commands.short_description: Specify contact group names.

commands.name: groupbehavior
commands.short_description: A contact is deemed to be in the range if both ends are within a group specified in the range

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List the energies of a contact model used by the contact

commands.name: model
commands.short_description: Assign contact models. The selected contact model applies to contacts in the specified range. If no range is specified, then all contacts in the model are affected.

commands.name: persist
commands.short_description: Set the contact persistence flag.

commands.name: property
commands.short_description: Assign contact properties

notes: PFC 5.0 supports contact model, cmat, and contact property commands

commands.name: add
commands.short_description: Specify the contact model

commands.name: apply
commands.short_description: Apply the CMAT to existing contacts. Visit each contact in the specified range and override its contact model according to the CMAT. Note that the contact models are entirely reassigned to each contact; all information stored in the previous contact model is lost

commands.name: default
commands.short_description: Specify the default model. The default slot(s) will not be modified if the corresponding contact type(s) cannot use the specified contact model

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: Lists the CMAT.

commands.name: modify
commands.short_description: Specify the contact model

notes: In PFC 5.0, 'cmat' is a standalone top-level command, NOT a subcommand of 'contact'

commands.name: attribute
commands.short_description: Force vector applied to clumps

commands.name: create
commands.short_description: This keyword specifies that the clump position, volume, and inertia tensor will be calculated from the pebble distribution assuming constant density. The space containing the clump is recursively subdivided into boxes that are {squares in 2D; cubes in 3D}. The {areas in 2D; volumes in 3D} and inertia tensors of all boxes that fall completely within any pebbles are accumulated. The parallel axis theorem is used to accumulate the inertia tensors. For each iteration the "final" volume is extrapolated, and the iterations cease when either 1) the total number of cells to be subdivided reaches 1e6 or 2) the extrapolated and estimated volumes are within the desired tolerance

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete clumps

commands.name: distribute
commands.short_description: Range of azimuths to rotate the clump template randomly about

commands.name: export
commands.short_description: Resolution of the facet edges for the pebble representations

commands.name: extra
commands.short_description: Set clump or pebble extra variables.

commands.name: fix
commands.short_description: The angular velocity is fixed

commands.name: free
commands.short_description: The angular velocity is free

commands.name: generate
commands.short_description: Range of azimuths to rotate the clump template randomly about

commands.name: group
commands.short_description: Specify clump or pebble group names.

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Magnitude of the force resulting from contacts

commands.name: initialize
commands.short_description: Modify clump attributes.

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List clump attributes

commands.name: list clump
commands.short_description: List clump information.

commands.name: order
commands.short_description: Set the rotational EOM order. Either two or four are acceptable values. The fourth order method is outlined in Johnson et al. (2008) while the second order method is outlined in Buss (2000) (augmented second order method without energy conservation). Energy is much better conserved with the fourth order scheme, but the computational cost is significantly higher.

commands.name: property
commands.short_description: Assign clump or pebble surface properties.

commands.name: replicate
commands.short_description: Angle about which to rotate the clump in degrees via the right-hand rule. If no angle is specified, then no rotation will occur. In 2D, rotation occurs in the out-of-plane direction

commands.name: result
commands.short_description: Activate the creation of clump result states in memory or as files

commands.name: rotate
commands.short_description: Axis of rotation

commands.name: scale
commands.short_description: Scale the clump to have volume of an equivalent {disk with unit thickness in 2D; sphere in 3D} with a specified diameter

commands.name: template
commands.short_description: Create a clump template. The inertial attributes must be specified upon creation. One can either specify them directly (i.e

commands.name: tolerance
commands.short_description: Assign the constant contact-detection tolerance. This is assigned on a per-pebble basis

commands.name: assemble
commands.short_description: ID of the brick being assembled

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete bricks in range

commands.name: export
commands.short_description: ID of the brick to export

commands.name: import
commands.short_description: Name of the file to be loaded. If not specified, then the file {"brick.p3brk" in 3D; "brick.p2brk" in 2D} is used. If no extension is given, the the extension {".p3brk" in 3D; ".p2brk" in 2D} is used

commands.name: create
commands.short_description: Measurement ID. If not specified, the next available ID is assigned

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete a measurement object. By default, all measurement objects are deleted. A range can be specified to restrict the operation to select objects.

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Coordination, or average number of contacts per body, inside the measurement object

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List information related to measurement regions

commands.name: modify
commands.short_description: Radius of the measurement circle (sphere)

commands.name: domain
commands.short_description: Set or modify the domain extent and condition

commands.name: plot
commands.short_description: Sets the destination file. If one is not specified, then one will be assigned. The extension determines the format (PNG by default). (PNG, JPG, BMP, PPM, and XBM formats are supported

commands.name: calm
commands.short_description: Calm the model. For mechanical processes, all linear and rotational velocities are set to 0

commands.name: clean
commands.short_description: Clean the model

commands.name: contact_history
commands.short_description: Magnitude of the contact force

commands.name: continue
commands.short_description: Resume processing after a pause. This command allows the user to resume reading a data file

commands.name: cycle
commands.short_description: Execute timesteps

commands.name: heading
commands.short_description: Set the model title. The title is included in subsequent plots and stored in model state (SAV) files

commands.name: history measure
commands.short_description: Add a measure history

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: Print information. Program and model information is printed to the screen

commands.name: list cmat
commands.short_description: Lists the CMAT

commands.name: list contact
commands.short_description: Lists contact information

commands.name: list measure
commands.short_description: List information related to measurement regions

commands.name: new
commands.short_description: Clear program-state information. Most information is cleared, allowing one to begin a new problem

commands.name: pause
commands.short_description: Pause command processing

commands.name: set
commands.short_description: Change a global setting

commands.name: solve
commands.short_description: Solve to a specified limit. The solve logic provides a mechanism to continue cycling a model until specified criteria are met, oftentimes resulting in a model that is in equilibrium to some tolerance

commands.name: step
commands.short_description: Execute timesteps

commands.name: stop
commands.short_description: Close the application

commands.name: title
commands.short_description: Set the model title. The title is included in subsequent plots and stored in model state (SAV) files

commands.name: call
commands.short_description: Process the specified data file(s)

commands.name: config
commands.short_description: Configure an add-on. This command allows the user to specify optional calculation modes that need extra memory to be assigned to specified model components

commands.name: echo
commands.short_description: Echo to the terminal

commands.name: exit
commands.short_description: Close the application

commands.name: gui
commands.short_description: Save the current project

commands.name: help
commands.short_description: Get command help

commands.name: mail
commands.short_description: Operate on email

commands.name: playback
commands.short_description: Play back a record file. An ASCII record file representing a "playback" of input to the model is read, theoretically exactly duplicating the steps taken to create a model state

commands.name: quit
commands.short_description: Close the application

commands.name: return
commands.short_description: Return program control. If in batch mode, the mode is set to interactive-command mode (if a single level of calling was used), or to the calling file (if multiple levels of calls have been nested)

commands.name: system
commands.short_description: Send commands to DOS. This command allows the user to access the underlying command-driven operating system (DOS, under Windows)

commands.name: undo
commands.short_description: Undo commands

commands.name: load
commands.short_description: Load a user-defined DLL

commands.name: restore
commands.short_description: Restore program-state information

commands.name: save
commands.short_description: Save all program-state information

notes: 'cmat' is a standalone command, not 'contact cmat'
Use 'new' instead of 'model new' to start a fresh model
ball attribute vs ball property: attribute=state, property=contact surface values
